title: Hub push carries owner identity + repo records (account shows up in Grove)
description: A remote user's 'bole push --as' to a hub must also deliver their signed Profile and RepoRecords into the hub's refs/collab/public/* store, so their account and repos appear under their profile in Grove. Today push only carries the timeline into refs/users/<fp>/; collab objects are denied on push, so a pushed repo never surfaces in Grove. The hub verifies each object is signed by the authenticated owner before landing it (owner can only publish their own identity/repos).

title: R3: bound tree-walk recursion + gate fetch landing (stack-overflow DoS)
description: AUDIT2 #1 HIGH. walk_tree_filtered recurses per tree level with no depth cap; a ~6MB chain of 100k nested single-entry trees overflows the worker stack -> SIGABRT, and persists (crash on every read). Add MAX_TREE_DEPTH cap returning Error (also in git_projection write_git_tree_level); enforce at ingest; gate client_fetch/http_fetch object landing. Files: src/repo/mod.rs, src/repo/git_projection.rs, src/sync/session.rs, src/sync/http.rs

title: R2: validate RefName at wire trust boundary (path traversal)
description: AUDIT2 #6 HIGH. RefName derives Deserialize, so a pushed ref name with ../ escapes <root>/refs on write (arbitrary-file create/clobber). Implement Deserialize for RefName routing through RefName::new; re-validate op.name in apply_push_ops + HTTP handler; add escape guard in ref_path (reject .././/NUL, assert final path under refs). Files: src/refs/name.rs, src/refs/disk.rs, src/sync/session.rs, src/sync/http.rs

title: Fix confined no-write-down bypass (can_write + eval_cap)
description: AUDIT #4 HIGH. writes_down uses global .all() over every clearance and ignores scope, so any second write clearance not strictly dominating the target flips deny->allow. Base decision on in-scope write-capable clearances whose ceiling equals the label; mirror in eval_cap; multi-clearance/scoped regression tests. File: src/acl/mod.rs:273-292,355-373

title: Wire policy engine into mutation paths + signed-approval resolver
description: AUDIT #3 HIGH. evaluate_replayable has no caller; advance_timeline/merge_timelines/apply_push_ops never run hooks; SignedApprovalHook unreachable from resolve_hook. Run deterministic registry on Advance, at merge-apply with real result head, and per accepted RefUpdateOp; add 'signed-approval' resolver arm; make hook gate Advance (or model merge-commit as Merge); deprecate forgeable ref-counting ApprovalHook. Files: src/repo/mod.rs, src/sync/session.rs, src/acl/hook.rs, src/acl/attestation.rs

title: serve_fetch: enforce read-ACL on served objects, not just advert
description: AUDIT #2 HIGH. serve_fetch passes client-supplied want raw to missing_closure/build_pack with no ACL re-check -> ref-granularity read control bypassed; read revocation ineffective. Intersect wants with closure reachable from accessor's authorized adverts, as the HTTP path already does. File: src/sync/session.rs:39-52

title: Bound decompression + wire buffering (zstd bomb, frame/header/body caps)
description: AUDIT #1 HIGH. decode_pack uses zstd::decode_all with no output cap (pre-auth OOM); deframe/TcpConn::recv/read_http buffer unbounded; O(n^2) find rescan. Cap per-object/per-frame/total-pack/frame-length-prefix/header-bytes/Content-Length; streaming decode aborting past declared ulen; fix find rescan. Files: src/store/pack.rs, src/sync/{transport,wire,http}.rs

title: Grove user directory: list everyone who has pushed to the hub
description: Add a public user directory so Grove has a browsable landing page instead of requiring a 64-hex key in the URL. Library: Repository::hub_users() = union of public_profiles and repo-record owners, each with display_name + repo_count. bole-api: GET /v1/users. Grove: home page lists them, linking to /u/<key>.

title: bole account create/show + fold profile/announce into push (one-command onboarding)
description: Add 'bole account create' (generate an ed25519 keypair = an account, write the seed to a keyfile 0600, print the account id) and 'bole account show'. Fold identity+listing into push: 'bole push <hub> <repo>:main --as key --name <display> --announce <desc>' publishes the profile + RepoRecord (monotonic seq) before pushing, so onboarding a new user to a hub is a couple of commands, not five.

title: Hub slice 3: owner-namespaced authenticated push (the crux)
description: Slice 3 of Grove-hub, the load-bearing security slice. The pusher signs an owner identity on the wire (reuse ed25519 + the Hello client_nonce/authn); the hub verifies it and builds an Accessor scoped to refs/users/<owner-fp>/** so apply_push_ops (already ACL-gated) refuses any write outside the owner's namespace. CLI: bole push <hub> --as <keyfile> <repo>:<timeline>. May split into 3a (wire+authn+scoped accessor) and 3b (CLI). Depends on slice 1. Localhost/trusted transport still (no TLS).

title: Hub slice 1: RepoRecord signed object + publish_repo/list_repos
description: First tracer-bullet slice of the Grove-hub epic. A signed content-addressed RepoRecord {owner: Key, name, description, seq, sig}, published under refs/collab/public/repo/<owner-fp>/<name>, verified fail-closed. Repository::publish_repo/list_repos(owner)/get_repo. Mirrors the Post/Profile object pattern. Library only.

title: [epic] Grove hub — multi-user repo hosting (push your repos, list under profile, others pull)
description: Grove is a hub: many bole users push their repos to a shared hub node, each profile lists all their repos, anyone can pull. Decisions (2026-07-12): one hub store namespaced by owner (refs/users/<owner-fp>/<repo>/<timeline>); authenticated ownership (only the owner-key holder writes their namespace). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-grove-hub-multi-user-repos-design.md. Decomposed into slices.

title: PR slice 2: bole pr create/list/show CLI + --json
description: Slice 2 of bole-j899. CLI over the ChangeProposal object (slice 1): 'bole pr create --from <src> --into <target>', 'bole pr list', 'bole pr show <id> --json'. Persist a proposal registry (name/id map) mirroring the secrets/actors registry pattern. Parent epic: bole-j899. Depends on slice 1.
notes: Review requirements carried from slice 1 (bole-060a): (1) MUST pin each proposal under a refs/proposals/... tag so it survives GC — put_proposal currently stores a GC-ephemeral leaf (mirror publish_profile's ref pinning). (2) Consider verify_strict (not verify) for proposals if the registry keys on ObjectId, to avoid signature-malleability producing two ids for one logical proposal (note: repo-wide verifiers all use non-strict verify today).

title: PR slice 1: ChangeProposal object — type + store round-trip + verify
description: First tracer-bullet slice of bole-j899 (PR system). Define a content-addressed ChangeProposal object referencing source + target timeline refs + author + created_at (+ optional signature like CollabObject). Store round-trip via ObjectStore; Repository::put/get_proposal. Library only, no CLI yet. Fail-closed verify if signed. Parent epic: bole-j899.

title: WS8b security: M2 scoped-ref gating + F4 publish TOCTOU
description: Deferred network-facing items from WS8a/b. M2: scoped/ refs must never leak via serve paths (gate them). F4: publish TOCTOU on profile/edge write. See ws8b spec.

title: WS1-O5: fail-closed on unknown PolicyHook kinds across replicas
description: Roadmap open question. Replicas that don't recognize a hook kind must fail closed, not open. Soundness of distributed policy. See roadmap-foundations + ws1 spec.

title: Grove dev landing page — render + serve Profile as a discoverable hub
description: API operation, NOT UI (Grove renders, later, separate repo). A profile-bundle READ query: aggregate a dev's Profile + their public repos/timelines + trust-graph slice into one JSON response. Cheapest product-surface API — all inputs exist (WS8a Profile + repo/timeline/trust APIs), needs an aggregating read op + --json. Grove consumes it.

title: Grove PR system — change-proposal + review-thread semantics
description: Headline product pillar, unbuilt. Build on timelines + acl approvals + the reserved TrustKind::Review edge (WS8a). A proposal references source/target timelines; review threads are objects; merge gates via existing PolicyHook/approval. Brainstorm->spec->plan. Biggest, highest-value surface.
notes: Decomposed into 5 tracer-bullet slices (2026-07-11): bole-060a (ChangeProposal object) -> bole-xwqv (pr CLI) -> bole-t290 (review threads) -> bole-ooxm (merge gating via approval) -> bole-4cnv (bole-api read endpoints). Slice 1 (bole-060a) is grabbable now; each slice blocks the next and the epic. Build brainstorm->spec->plan per slice when picked up.

title: git export empty for a bound actor: project_to_git uses wrong timeline visibility
description: project_to_git filters timelines via accessor.can_read_timeline() (accessor's own rules, NO public/bottom short-circuit), so a bound actor with only path grants cannot see an UNPROTECTED (bottom-label) timeline -> git export produces an empty repo (no refs/objects). list_refs_filtered (used by sync advertise) does it correctly: label from REPO rules + bottom short-circuit. Fix: project_to_git should use repo.list_refs_filtered("", accessor) for timeline visibility. Reported via bole git export --to on a CLI repo with a bound path-only actor.

title: Fix skills/README: in-memory is a co-equal backend, not a footnote
description: Skills lead 'Using the bole CLI' and line 33 states 'There is no in-memory/session mode' as a blanket fact — false about the library (only true of CLI-per-invocation). bole is co-equal dual-backend (in-memory Repository::memory()+EphemeralWorkspace AND disk CLI over .bole/). Reframe skills (claude-code/hermes/codex) to present both backends as peers; fix the misleading line; promote in-memory from bottom footnote to a co-equal section. Check README positioning too.

title: R1: serialize ref compare-and-swap (lost updates / FF bypass)
description: AUDIT2 #3 HIGH. commit_transaction runs resolve()+apply_atomic() unserialized, so concurrent pushers lose updates and bypass the FF/CAS gate. Hold a per-RefStore mutex across the whole commit_transaction; OS advisory lock on disk .txn dir for cross-process. Files: src/refs/mod.rs, src/refs/transaction.rs, src/refs/disk.rs

title: R4: advance_timeline compare-and-swap (depends on R1)
description: AUDIT2 #4 MED. advance_timeline uses unconditional advance_head (no CAS): loses concurrent winners, evaluates policy against stale head. Commit through advance_head_if(expected_old=timeline.head); anchor policy eval to same old head. File: src/repo/mod.rs

title: Fix cargo-audit advisories in gix transitive deps
description: cargo audit: RUSTSEC-2025-0021 (gix-features 0.40.0, SHA-1 collision detection missing, medium 6.8) and RUSTSEC-2025-0140 (gix-date 0.9.4, non-utf8 TimeBuf::as_str) — both transitive via gix 0.70 (git import/export paths). Plus unmaintained warnings atomic-polyfill (RUSTSEC-2023-0089), proc-macro-error2 (RUSTSEC-2026-0173). Bump gix to a version pulling gix-features>=0.41.0 and gix-date>=0.12.0; re-run cargo audit.

title: Replace glob_matches with linear/DP matcher + load-time limits
description: AUDIT #8 MED. Catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) on attacker-influenced patterns/paths. Memoize on (pat_idx,s_idx) or iterative two-pointer; cap pattern/path length and ** count; reject pathological patterns at policy load. File: src/acl/glob.rs

title: Threat model doc + unaudited README caveat
description: AUDIT #6 HIGH (fix-item F). docs/THREAT_MODEL.md stating adversary/scope; README 'experimental, unaudited, do not expose a network listener' caveat. Container for latent scaffold risks (unauth transports, unloaded lattices, dead signed-ref/authn code).

title: advance_timeline: write-check path deletions (symmetric diff)
description: AUDIT #7 MED. Only new tree is walked, so removing a protected path the actor cannot write is uncaught. Diff old head vs new snapshot; check can_write over symmetric difference. File: src/repo/mod.rs:508-574

title: Adversarial/negative test suite for verify + enforcement
description: AUDIT #5 HIGH (fix-item C). Negative tests for verify_chain/verify_attestation/count_valid_approvals, multi-clearance/scoped can_write, and end-to-end protected-merge-denied-without-N-attestations. Acceptance evidence for the enforcement fixes.

title: explain_path: decision-trace API for access decisions
description: Surface the existing lattice access logic as repo.explain_path(actor, snapshot, path) -> decision trace (allow/deny + reason chain: label_for_path, dominance, confined no-write-down, matched rule). Attacks the lattice-usability risk. Logic already exists in can_read/can_write; just needs a trace-producing wrapper + CLI verb.

title: WS5 follow-up: policy authority (TrustStore + PolicyVerifier + signed chain)
description: Deferred from WS5 (bole-cy6). Add sync::authority: TrustStore (per-repo trusted signing keys), PolicyVerifier (verify offered PolicyRoot chain to a TrustAnchor-signed root, fail-closed on unsigned/untrusted/unknown-hook/broken-chain), highest-rooted-wins conflict resolution, PolicyOffer transfer + TOFU on clone. Fills WS1's reserved Grant.signature semantics. Supersedes/subsumes bole-fz1 (signed approval attestation). See spec ws5 section 5. O2 direct-anchor v1.
close_reason: Policy authority complete (WS5 5). Ed25519 detached signatures (ed25519-dalek), TrustStore/TrustAnchor, PolicySigner (seed/KMS-swappable), verify_chain (fail-closed: present + lattice/rules present + hooks resolvable + direct-anchor signed), reconcile (highest-rooted-wins / Diverged). Full workspace green: 22 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. 3 tests (sign+verify, unsigned/untrusted/bad-hook reject, longer-chain-wins/divergent). Unblocks bole-fz1. Commit 6b88795.

title: WS5 follow-up: wire codec + Transport trait + HTTP/SSH transports
description: Deferred from WS5 (bole-cy6). Add sync::wire (Message enum + length-prefixed postcard frame codec + CapSet/version handshake), sync::transport (Transport/Conn trait, InProcessTransport, HttpTransport v1, SshTransport), and sync::session SyncSession state machine driving fetch/push over the trait. Makes the in-process core engine genuinely transport-agnostic + networked. See spec 2026-06-29-ws5 sections 3,7. O1: HTTP first.
close_reason: Sync wire codec + Transport/Conn trait + InProcessConn + SyncSession state machine (serve/client_fetch/client_push) complete. Protocol driven over any Conn, reusing WS4 packs + RefTransaction CAS + WS1 authz. Full workspace green: 22 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. 5 new tests (3 wire, 2 session over InProcess). No new deps. Concrete TCP/HTTP/SSH transports = bole-vih. Commits f746431.

title: WS4 follow-up: atomic ref transactions (RefTransaction + journal)
description: Deferred from WS4 (bole-81z). Add Repository::transaction() -> RefTransaction with buffered create_tag/move_tag/create_timeline/advance_head/delete_ref + CAS preconditions (expect, advance_head_if), committing all-or-nothing via a write-ahead journal over the existing per-file ref layout (refs/.txn/<txid>.journal recording absolute final ref values; commit point = journal fsync; idempotent replay on open()). Repo lock refs/.txn/lock serializes commits and excludes GC planning/retire. See spec 2026-06-29-ws4 section 8. Decision: journal-over-per-file (not packed-refs).
close_reason: Atomic multi-ref transactions complete. RefTransaction builder (create_tag/move_tag/create_timeline/advance_head/delete_ref + expect/advance_head_if CAS), all-or-nothing commit via absolute-value plan, journal-over-per-file on disk (fsync commit point + idempotent replay on open), Error::TransactionConflict. Full workspace green: 21 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. Unblocks WS5 CAS-on-heads. Commits 43500d5.

title: WS4: storage at scale (packs + index + GC + atomic refs)
description: Pack format (designed as the wire payload for WS5), repack, mark-sweep GC, atomic multi-ref transactions. See spec 2026-06-29-ws4.
close_reason: WS4 storage-at-scale complete (scope: pack format + PackedDiskBackend + repack + mark-sweep GC + count/compact; atomic ref transactions deferred to bole-sk6). Pack .pack/.idx self-verifying (WS5 wire payload), loose-first packed backend, crash-safe repack, GC from ref roots + extra_roots (registry-safe) with grace window, bole store repack/gc CLI. Full workspace green: 21 test binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. Decisions: plans/2026-07-01-ws4-decisions.md. Commits 4a15426..cd0de39.

title: WS5: distributed (remote/fetch/push, sync protocol)
description: Transport-agnostic sync over content-addressed packs; CAS on timeline heads; policy/label authority model. Needs WS1+WS4. See spec 2026-06-29-ws5.
close_reason: WS5 core sync engine complete (scope: in-process fetch/push/clone + CAS; wire codec/Transport/HTTP=bole-6qy, policy verification=bole-0tp, authn=bole-6h7 deferred). Negotiated missing-closure transfer via self-verifying WS4 packs, remote-tracking refs, git-model CAS push (expected_old=tracking ref) FF/policy gated, clone. Full workspace green: 22 test binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. Decisions: plans/2026-07-01-ws5-decisions.md. Commits 0d27e62.

title: WS1: hybrid access/policy core (labels + clearances + hook)
description: Replace glob-ACL 'lattice' with a real hybrid model: label lattice foundation, glob ACLs as a degenerate case, programmable PolicyHook. Unifies actor/acl/accessor into one pipeline. See spec 2026-06-29-ws1.
close_reason: WS1 hybrid access/policy core complete: 10/10 TDD tasks. Label lattice + rules + clearances + Accessor rewrite + Object::Policy + AclBackend shims + PolicyHook/registry + repo integration + ApprovalHook. All 247 prior tests preserved; full suite 191 lib + integration green. Commits 4576e7f..e8af940.

title: fix(repo): check_merge resolve snapshot to tree root
description: check_merge passes source_head (snapshot ObjectId) directly to walk_tree_filtered which expects a tree ObjectId. Must resolve snapshot to get snap.root before walking.

title: Gate 3 fix: check_merge always-Allowed bug + bead tags
description: check_merge passes empty Accessor to walk_tree_filtered so protected paths are never collected. Fix by walking with privileged all-paths accessor. Also fix retroactive bole-1vi tag and missing bole-9by on lib.rs.

title: Gate 1 fixes: blocking exists, tokio features, atomic writes
description: Three fixes from final code review: (1) DiskBackend::exists uses blocking Path::exists() — replace with tokio::fs::try_exists; (2) Cargo.toml has tokio features=full in dependencies — replace with minimal features; (3) DiskBackend::put is non-atomic on crash — use write-then-rename pattern

title: Hub slice 4: pull a repo (bole clone/fetch owner/repo) + Grove pull hint
description: Slice 4 of Grove-hub. bole clone/fetch a specific owner/repo from the hub (public read of refs/users/<owner-fp>/<repo>/**); Grove shows the pull command per repo. Depends on slices 1+3.

title: Hub slice 5: bole-api hub endpoints for repos
description: Slice 5 of Grove-hub. GET /v1/users/{key}/repos and GET /v1/repos/{owner}/{name} for Grove to render repo pages. Depends on slices 1+2.

title: Hub slice 2: profile lists repos (bundle + bole-api + Grove profile page + repo page)
description: Slice 2 of Grove-hub. Extend profile_bundle to include repos[] (the owner's RepoRecords); /v1/profiles/{key}/bundle carries them; Grove profile page lists repos; a Grove repo page /repo/{owner}/{name} shows the RepoRecord + link to its timelines. Depends on slice 1.

title: Wire bole native repo sync into the CLI: bole serve + bole push/fetch/clone over TCP
description: The distributed sync (Repository::push/fetch/clone_from + session::serve wire protocol + TcpConn) is built and tested at the library level but NOT CLI-wired — 'bole node serve' is collab-only. Surfaced concretely by Grove (dogfooding: push the Grove repo to a bole server). Add: 'bole serve --listen <addr>' (general repo serve for fetch+push, accessor-gated) and 'bole push <addr> <timeline...>' / 'bole fetch <addr>' over TCP, reusing the existing library sync. Localhost/trusted-network only (no TLS/peer auth per threat model).

title: bole-api: GET /v1/profiles/{key}/bundle — expose profile_bundle over HTTP (Grove gap #1)
description: Grove gap #1 (see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-11-grove-frontend-hub-design.md). Repository::profile_bundle exists (library) and 'bole profile bundle' (CLI), but there is no HTTP endpoint. Add GET /v1/profiles/{key}/bundle returning the profile-bundle JSON contract (key, is_local, profile-or-null, trust.edges[], timelines[]) reusing RequestAuth->Accessor (pass the caller's accessor so timelines are ACL-filtered per bole-e78l, NOT privileged) + the error envelope. The single missing piece for a complete read-only Grove landing page. Small.

title: PR slice 4: merge gating via existing PolicyHook/approval
description: Slice 4 of bole-j899. Wire a ChangeProposal 'merge' to the existing approval machinery: merging the proposal's source into its target goes through check_merge + the signed-approval PolicyHook (bole-6i7) so a PR into release/** needs N approvals. Reuses advance_timeline/check_merge; no new gating engine. Parent epic: bole-j899.
notes: Review (approve): security claims all confirmed — no approval-gate bypass, no ref-name injection (RefName + type-safe get_timeline block policy-Tag targets), no per-path ACL bypass, no partial application on conflict. Non-blocking: merge TOCTOU filed separately; orphan merge snapshot on gated-merge is GC-collectable (acceptable); conflict metadata unfiltered by read-ACL (inherent to merge_timelines).

title: PR slice 3: review-thread objects — comment + resolve
description: Slice 3 of bole-j899. Content-addressed review-comment objects attached to a ChangeProposal (author, body, created_at, optional resolve marker), signed + fail-closed verify. Repository::add_comment/list_comments. Uses the reserved TrustKind::Review edge where relevant. Parent epic: bole-j899.

title: bole-api write endpoints (advance/merge/publish) for Grove
description: Follow-up to bole-yd56: bole-api ships read-only today. When Grove needs to drive writes over HTTP, add write endpoints (timeline advance, merge, secret/profile publish) reusing the same RequestAuth->Accessor path and error envelope. Gated on Grove's stack/needs being known; the signed-request auth (bole-e333) and ACL plumbing are already in place. Deferred per mission: Grove is a later, separate-repo concern.

title: Verified policy-root adoption from a remote: wire verify_chain into an explicit adopt step; CAS + parent-chain (anti-rollback) + trust-anchor check in set_policy_root
description: Follow-up to bole-au0t (WS1-O5). Today a replica enforces a pinned policy root fail-closed, but there is no verified path to ADOPT a root fetched from a peer: set_policy_root is an unauthenticated, unversioned upsert (no CAS on the previous pin, no requirement that the new root's parent chain contain the old one, no TrustAnchor signature check despite verify_chain existing in acl/authority.rs), and fetch/clone deliberately never auto-adopt. Wire verify_chain + reconcile into an explicit 'adopt from remote-tracking ref' operation, add anti-rollback (parent-chain containment) and optional signature enforcement to set_policy_root, and consider an Expect op on refs/policy/root in advance_timeline's ref transaction to close the registry/CAS TOCTOU noted in the bole-au0t review.
notes: Review residuals from bole-au0t + bole-e78l (non-blocking, local trust domain): add the reserved-namespace filter (refs/policy/* AND refs/collab/*) to in-process Repository::push for symmetry with apply_push_ops — a local caller can currently create a timeline at those names on the destination, wedging policy_root()/collab_adverts. Also: pushed objects land before per-op policy denials on both push paths (pre-existing).

title: bole-api: decide unknown-bearer-token contract (anonymous vs 401) and test it
description: An unmapped bearer token silently becomes Anonymous; design text said bad token -> 401. Pick one behavior, document, and test. From bole-3xj5 final review.

title: bole-api: point-lookup ACL check for timelines get_one (avoid O(all-refs) scan)
description: get_one calls list_refs_filtered (full scan) just to test membership then refs.get. Add a direct can-read-timeline check for point lookups. Correctness/security fine; perf only. From bole-3xj5 re-review.

title: bole-api: signed-auth hardening (keyId enumeration oracle + dual-stack proxy IP)
description: 1) Collapse all signed-arm failures to one generic 401 and check skew before key lookup, so error text can't distinguish unknown-keyId from bad-sig. 2) Parse trusted-proxy entries to IpAddr and compare normalized (IPv4-mapped-IPv6). Both fail-closed today. From bole-3xj5 final review.

title: bole-api: gate debug_auth_router/principal_kind behind cfg(test) or feature
description: router::debug_auth_router and auth::principal_kind are pub and compile into the shipped lib (not mounted at runtime). Put behind a testing feature; must stay reachable from tests/api.rs (separate crate). From bole-3xj5 final review.

title: bole-api: bind query string in signed-request canonical message (GATE before first write endpoint)
description: Signed-request canonical string binds METHOD+PATH but NOT the query string, so ?path= etc are mutable in transit. Safe now (read-only + ACL), but MUST fix before any write endpoint or authorization-relevant query param. From bole-3xj5 final review.

title: bole-api: all error responses use JSON envelope (unmatched routes + extractor rejections)
description: axum default 404 for unmatched routes and 400/404 for Query/Path extractor rejections return bare bodies, not the {error:{code,message}} envelope. Add Router::fallback + handle extractor rejections. From bole-3xj5 final review.

title: bole-api: walking-skeleton read API server (axum)
description: Implement docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-04-bole-api-server-skeleton-design.md. New bole-api workspace crate: axum HTTP/JSON server, read-only endpoints (status/repos/timelines/snapshots/objects/profiles) over existing lib ops. Auth = extract Principal from HTTP (bearer token, mTLS-via-trusted-proxy-header, ed25519 signed request) then reuse authn::accessor_for for ACL. JSON error envelope, ACL-hidden -> 404, /v1 versioning. In-process oneshot tests.

title: bole ignore check: false negatives under ignored dirs + trailing-slash paths
description: `bole ignore check` uses Gitignore::matched(path,is_dir), which only tests the exact path and does not model the walk's parent-directory pruning. Files under an ignored directory (e.g. target/out.bin when target/ is ignored) and trailing-slash query paths (target/) are reported 'not ignored', contradicting the actual snapshot walk. Fix: use matched_path_or_any_parents and normalize trailing slashes so check is a faithful dry-run. Found dogfooding Grove. Introduced in bole-phxz.

title: bole ignore: .boleignore file + gitignore matcher + CLI
description: Implement ignore mechanism per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-04-bole-ignore-design.md. Tracked .boleignore at workspace root, gitignore-compatible matcher (ignore crate) enforced in DiskWorkspace::collect (prune dirs / skip files), new 'bole ignore' CLI with add(default)/list/remove/check. .bole and symlinks stay hard-excluded.

title: Decide MultiRecipientSecret: wire to a feature or remove (secrets consolidation)
description: Scope-audit finding: MultiRecipientSecret (per-recipient DK wrapping) is defined, persistable via the store, re-exported, and shown in 'object' inspection, but NO repo/env/CLI feature creates or consumes it (unlike Secret=55 refs and SecretV2=23 refs which are load-bearing). It's the clearest 'built ahead of need' capability. DECIDE: either wire it to a real per-recipient-secret workflow, or remove it and collapse the secrets story to Secret + SecretV2. Do not leave a third scheme no consumer drives. See docs/superpowers/SCOPE-AUDIT.md.

title: bole API-server transport (HTTP/JSON-RPC) for the Grove frontend
description: No product-facing API server exists today — only the Rust library, CLI --json, and the p2p node-serve wire protocol. Grove (separate frontend repo, later) needs a network API (likely HTTP/JSON-RPC) over bole's operations. RECOMMENDATION: defer the concrete server until Grove's stack/needs are known; meanwhile keep every domain op JSON-serializable so the veneer is thin. Or stand up a minimal 'bole serve --api' to pin the contract early. Decision bead.

Remaining gap (write endpoints) is Grove-driven future work, tracked separately and deferred per the mission (Grove is a later, separate-repo concern).

Directed reverse-reachability ball: per match BFS backward to_key->from_key up to max_hops, collect edges; union across matches deduped by object-id (edge_id via codec::serialize+object_id). title: CLI: linked worktrees (workspace add/list/remove)

Discovery in context.rs handles .bole-dir (primary, cli-state.json) vs .bole-file (linked). New commands: workspace add <path> --timeline <name> [--as actor] (timeline must exist; materialize head), workspace list (primary + linked: path/timeline/head), workspace remove <path> (unregister + delete pointer/metadata, never user files). Warn-but-allow binding a timeline already used by another worktree. Backward compatible with existing single-worktree repos.

title: Enforce TimelinePolicy in advance_timeline
description: Repository::advance_timeline (src/repo/mod.rs ~line 279) checks timeline and path write ACLs but never consults the timeline's TimelinePolicy before calling refs.advance_head. As a result FastForwardOnly and Append are stored but not enforced: a timeline created with --policy ff can be advanced to a non-descendant snapshot. The CLI faithfully stores the policy (bole-cli), so the gap is purely in the library.
design:
Load the target Timeline, read its policy, and gate the advance on the new head's relationship to the current head BEFORE refs.advance_head:
- FastForwardOnly: reject unless current head is an ancestor of new head (reuse the existing LCA/ancestry helper used by merge, e.g. find_common_ancestor: new head is a fast-forward iff lca(current, new) == current).
- Append: reject unless current head is an ancestor of new head (same descendant check; arbitrary rewrites disallowed). Distinguish from ff only if/when a weaker rule is defined; otherwise treat both as 'must descend from current head'.
- Unrestricted: no ancestry constraint (current behaviour).
Return Error::WrongRefKind or a new Error variant (e.g. Error::PolicyViolation) with a clear message. Decide whether advance_head (raw RefStore setter) stays policy-free (it should: it is the low-level primitive; enforcement lives in the ACL/policy-aware Repository layer).
acceptance_criteria: 1. advance_timeline on a ff/append timeline returns an error when the new head is not a descendant of the current head. 2. advance_timeline on a ff/append timeline succeeds for a true fast-forward. 3. Unrestricted timelines are unaffected. 4. Unit tests in src/repo cover ff-reject, ff-accept, append-reject, and unrestricted-allow. 5. Existing 222 workspace tests still pass; CLI timeline advance still works for unrestricted (its default).

title: Hub confidentiality: decide/document that hub repo metadata (names+heads) is public by default
description: From the bole-1x2v review. On a default hub, all refs/users/<fp>/** refs are unlabeled (lattice bottom = world-readable), so the accessor read-scope is dead for confidentiality — any peer can read every owner's repo/timeline names and head ids. The push handshake was tightened (bole-1x2v) to advertise only the pusher's own namespace, but the general read is still public. For the hub model this is arguably INTENDED (others pull public repos), but it should be an explicit decision + documented, and slices 4/5 (pull + hub API) should either keep it public-by-design or add per-owner read labels if privacy is wanted. Foundational for how public a hub is.

title: Merge TOCTOU: diff computed against a target head that a concurrent advance can move before the CAS
description: From review of bole-ooxm (also affects the shipped 'bole merge run' path). merge_timelines computes a three-way diff against the target head, then advance_timeline does its CAS against its OWN fresh read of the target head. If a concurrent writer advances the target between the diff and the advance read, the CAS still succeeds and commits a merge tree computed against stale target state (stale first parent, silently losing the concurrent commit). Not an approval/ACL bypass (the gate runs against the fresh head). Fix: thread the diff-time target head into advance as the expected_old (a merge-specific advance_if), so a moved target conflicts instead of clobbering. Pre-existing; low priority.

title: bole-api sync: Repository::push non-deterministic-policy early return mislabels all timelines as policy-denied
description: From code-review of the bole-au0t batch. In src/sync/mod.rs Repository::push, the non-deterministic-registry guard does 'return Ok(timelines.iter().map(|name| Denied(non-deterministic reason)))' BEFORE per-timeline validation. So a timeline the caller does not own, or that does not exist locally, is reported with the 'peer binds non-deterministic policy hook(s)' reason instead of the accurate 'no such local timeline' / 'write denied on timeline'. Misleading status only — the action (refuse) is correct and safe. Fix: either keep the blanket refusal but use a neutral reason, or validate ownership/existence per timeline first. Purely a status-accuracy cleanup.

title: bole-api: confirm/covered — Authorization header short-circuits mTLS+anonymous fallthrough (intended 261x behavior)
description: From code-review of bole-261x. In bole-api/src/auth.rs resolve_principal, any Authorization header (recognized scheme or not) now returns before the x-bole-client-subject trusted-proxy mTLS check. A client sending BOTH an Authorization header and a trusted-proxy mTLS subject can no longer reach the mTLS arm. This is the INTENDED 261x contract (a presented credential must resolve or 401), so this bead is a verify-and-document task, not a fix: add a test pinning the precedence (Authorization wins over x-bole-client-subject), and a README line stating the resolution order is strict — the first presented credential decides, it does not fall through to a later arm. Close as wont-fix-code if the test + doc confirm current behavior is desired.

title: bole-api: ApiPath/ApiQuery should preserve extractor rejection status, not flatten all to 400
description: From code-review of the bole-api batch. bole-api/src/extract.rs maps EVERY Path/Query rejection to ApiError::bad_request (400). axum's Path MissingPathParams rejection is a route/extractor arity mismatch — a server programmer error that should be 500, not a client 400. Low impact today because all handlers use String params (whose realistic rejection genuinely is 400), but it will mislabel any future typed-param handler and masks a server bug as client error. Fix: match on the rejection kind (or rejection.status()) and map MissingPathParams/deserialize-of-wrong-arity to internal(500), percent-decode/UTF-8/query-deserialize failures to 400. Also avoid leaking serde detail via rej.to_string() in the client-facing message. Overlaps bole-rnpe item (4).
notes: Overlaps bole-rnpe item (4) which also covers the ApiPath status-fidelity + serde-detail-leak cleanup. Do together or dedupe.

title: bole-api auth/error hardening follow-ups from batch review
description: Non-blocking findings from the bole-261x..tgr8 batch review: (1) timing oracle in signed arm — unknown keyId returns before the ed25519 verify while bad-sig pays hash+verify; add a dummy verify on the unknown-key path. (2) untrusted-proxy x-bole-client-subject silently anonymizes — add a log line so a missing/unparseable [proxy].trusted entry is diagnosable. (3) README: add a troubleshooting note for the generic 'signed request rejected' 401 (covers missing header/stale date/bad sig/unknown key). (4) ApiPath flattens MissingPathParams (a 500-class programmer error) to 400 — match on rejection kind; avoid leaking serde detail via rej.to_string(). (5) ref_served: hidden-vs-absent is status-identical but not time-identical (absent short-circuits before ACL eval); evaluate label gate unconditionally if timing ever matters. (6) extract a shared private helper for the bottom||can_read label rule now duplicated across list_refs_filtered/ref_served.

notes: Frozen per SCOPE-AUDIT + HANDOFF (2026-07-04): stop deepening the discovery/VCS substrate — it is over-provisioned for the mission. Deferred, not closed; reopen if a product feature (Grove) actually pulls it. See docs/superpowers/SCOPE-AUDIT.md.

title: Grove message board — discussion/board objects + surface
description: Headline pillar, unbuilt. Signed discussion objects (like CollabObject) + a board surface. Depends on nothing beyond the collab substrate; lower priority than PR/landing.
notes: Decomposed into 3 tracer-bullet slices (2026-07-11): bole-lkv3 (signed post object) -> bole-6m6f (board CLI), bole-p0lo (bole-api read endpoint). Slice 1 (bole-lkv3) grabbable now. Lower priority than PR system per the epic.

title: CLI porcelain for signed approvals (approver add / approve / policy config)
description: Follow-up to bole-6i7 (library slice). Add CLI: 'bole approver add <key-id> --key-file/--seed' (register an approver in the content-addressed registry), 'bole approve <timeline> <head>' (bound approver signs a head-bound attestation), and signed-approval policy configuration ('bole policy set-approval <pattern> --needed N' or via HookSpec). Plus CLI integration tests. Depends on bole-6i7.

title: R7: unique ref journal/temp filenames (concurrent collision)
description: AUDIT2 #5 LOW. disk journal txid=blake3(bytes); identical concurrent plans collide on journal+temp paths (spurious errors + delete window). Use {txid}-{nonce}.journal + O_EXCL temp. File: src/refs/disk.rs. Largely subsumed by R1.

title: R6: validate worktree pointer id / registry keys
description: AUDIT2 #7 LOW. ptr.id used unsanitised building state paths (../.. escapes store). Reject ids not matching allocate_id charset; assert resolved paths under <store>/worktrees. Files: bole-cli/src/context.rs, bole-cli/src/worktrees.rs

title: Wire MultiRecipientSecret into Object model + CLI
description: bole-21g delivered the multi-recipient envelope crypto primitive (MultiRecipientSecret: encrypt_for/decrypt/grant/revoke, per-actor DK wraps). Remaining integration: add an Object::MultiRecipientSecret variant (new variant, not a SecretV2 field change — postcard is not self-describing), store put/get helpers, per-actor key resolution in the CLI, and porcelain 'secret grant-actor <name> <actor>' / 'secret revoke-actor'. Also decide storage of actor->key_ref mapping. Depends on bole-21g.

title: Actor-scoped key wrapping for secrets
description: Today all cleared actors unwrap via the same master key. Extend envelope encryption so data keys can be wrapped per-actor (or per-label), rotation tied to labels/timelines. Design-level; larger than the others.

title: WS7 Part B: full doc repositioning (CLI.md, API.md, bole-cli README, skills)
description: Deferred from WS7 (bole-3hj). Complete the Part B narrative rewrite per spec 2026-06-29-ws7 section 3.4: docs/CLI.md mental model + linked-worktrees section, docs/API.md core-concepts + Access model subsection, bole-cli/README.md opening + worktree row, and skills/{claude-code/SKILL.md, codex/AGENTS.md, hermes/SKILL.md} description+opening+critical-facts (kept in sync). Lead with the access-control differentiator; mark WS1-6 realized (they shipped). README positioning + roadmap table already done in bole-3hj. Also the multi-actor worked example script (tests/example_multi_actor.sh).
close_reason: WS7 Part B doc repositioning complete. CLI.md (expanded mental model + linked-worktree hardening section), API.md (Core-concepts framing + Access model subsection), bole-cli/README (repositioned opening + Worktree row), skills claude-code/hermes/codex (new description + access-inside-repo addendum + --as/prune-repair critical facts; two SKILL.md kept identical). All lead with the access-control differentiator; WS1-6 framed as realized. Deferred: runnable multi-actor example script (needs aspirational acl/actor CLI verbs). Commit 9e47dfa.

title: Reconcile WS1 spec: document async PolicyHook::check deviation
description: WS1 implementation made PolicyHook::check async (via async_trait) because timeline fast-forward ancestry walks need ObjectStore::get().await. The WS1 spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-29-ws1-access-policy-core.md) sketches it as sync. Update the spec text to match the shipped async signature so future readers aren't misled. Implementation plan already notes the deviation.
close_reason: WS1 spec PolicyHook::check sketch updated to the shipped async signature + rationale (async_trait; ancestry walks need get().await). Doc-only. Commit.

title: Clean up pre-existing unused-import warnings in src/repo/mod.rs
description: Pre-existing warnings (not from WS1/WS2) across the workspace: (1) src/repo/mod.rs:17 imports PathRole+Permission used only in #[cfg(test)] modules with local imports; (2) ObjectId imported unused in ~3 repo/mod.rs test-module use statements; (3) bole-cli field worktree_id never read. Clean these up for a warning-free 'cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets'.
close_reason: Cleared all pre-existing warnings: repo/mod.rs module-level PathRole/Permission + 3 unused ObjectId test imports removed; worktree_id marked allow(dead_code). cargo build --workspace is now warning-free; clippy clean; 23 test binaries green. Commit.

title: Skills: add EphemeralWorkspace note
description: The skills/ guides (claude-code, codex, hermes) already cover linked worktrees, policy enforcement, and glob semantics. Add a short note about the library's in-memory EphemeralWorkspace (Repository::memory() + ephemeral_workspace()) for in-process agents, pointing to docs/API.md.

title: Add agent skills for using the bole CLI
description: Create skills/ guides teaching agents to drive the bole CLI for version control: skills/claude-code/SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter), skills/codex/AGENTS.md (Codex format), skills/hermes/SKILL.md (frontmatter). Content covers the mental model, core workflow, command groups, and gotchas; links to docs/CLI.md and bole-cli/README.md.

title: Add MIT license
description: Add a LICENSE file (MIT, Leslie Russell, 2026), set license="MIT" in both Cargo.toml manifests, and update the README License section from TBD to MIT.

title: Docs: bole-cli/README.md
description: Add a crate-level README for bole-cli covering every command and flag with multiple worked workflow examples.

title: Docs: CLI reference + README update
description: Add docs/CLI.md command reference for the bole binary and update README to reflect that a CLI now ships (correcting the 'not a CLI' line) with a CLI quick start.

title: CLI: plumbing (object/ref/store) + repo info
description: Add low-level plumbing: object (list/get/type/cat/put-blob), ref (list/get/delete), store (stats/fsck), and repo info. Wraps ObjectStore/RefStore directly. No src/ changes.

title: G7: project_to_git doesn't set HEAD to a projected branch
description: gix::init_bare sets HEAD to gix default branch name. If the bole repo's only timeline isn't named that default, a bare 'git log' reports 'no commits' even though refs/heads/<name> is valid. project_to_git should write HEAD to point at one of the projected branches.

title: Strengthen nonce uniqueness test to assert distinct ObjectIds
description: test two_encryptions_have_different_nonces in src/object/secret.rs only asserts nonce inequality at the struct level. The security-relevant property is that two put_secret calls with identical plaintext produce distinct ObjectIds (proving no equality leakage through the content-addressed store). Add a store-level test asserting distinct IDs. Discovered during Gate 4 final whole-branch review.

title: check_merge returns Allowed for missing/non-snapshot source ref
description: When the source ref is missing or its head is not a Snapshot object, check_merge returns MergeCheck::Allowed rather than Rejected or an error. For a security gate this may be wrong — a non-resolvable source arguably warrants rejection.

title: DiskAclBackend: sanitize should escape % to prevent ACL collision
description: sanitize() escapes / and * but not %. Globs 'a/b' and 'a%2Fb' both map to filename 'a%2Fb', causing a silent ACL overwrite. Fix: escape % first (to %25), then / and *.

title: DiskBackend::list swallows shard open errors
description: In src/store/disk.rs DiskBackend::list(), shard-directory open failures are silently swallowed (Err(_) => continue) while entry enumeration errors propagate via ?. If a shard is unreadable due to permissions, copy_to would produce a silently incomplete copy. Recommend distinguishing NotFound (skip) from other errors (propagate), mirroring how delete/the outer read_dir handle it.

title: Untagged new-code blocks in src/repo/mod.rs
description: The 'use' imports + struct Repository block and the #[cfg(test)] mod tests block in src/repo/mod.rs are missing // bole-1vi bead comments. Per-task reviews fixed similar issues in set_raw and materialize test block but these were missed. Add // bole-1vi before each untagged contiguous block.

title: MemoryBackend: use ObjectId as HashMap key instead of [u8;32]
description: MemoryBackend stores Bytes keyed by [u8; 32] but ObjectId already derives Hash + Eq and can be used directly. Using the raw byte array discards the type-safety invariant. Replace HashMap<[u8;32], Bytes> with HashMap<ObjectId, Bytes>.

title: ObjectId: add FromStr impl (parse hex string)
description: Display emits 64-char lowercase hex but there is no FromStr. The CLI will need to parse ObjectIds from user input and config. Implement FromStr that parses a 64-char hex string back into ObjectId.

title: t6_agent_capability_enforced: add intent comment
description: Add a comment in the t6_agent_capability_enforced test explaining that passing the same snapshot as the current head is intentional. Guards against a future same-head short-circuit optimization in advance_timeline silently skipping path-level enforcement.

title: check_merge: use Accessor::privileged() instead of inline
description: Refactor the ad-hoc Accessor construction inside check_merge (bole-hc1 block) to use the new Accessor::privileged() constructor added in Gate 6. Currently builds inline with with_path_role, which is functionally identical but inconsistent with the new helper.

title: merge tests: add dedicated lca_diamond test
description: Add a test named lca_diamond that explicitly covers the diamond DAG topology (base → L/R → tip). The topology is currently tested indirectly but has no explicitly named test.

title: find_common_ancestor: remove dead overlap guards
description: Remove the two permanently-false 'immediate overlap' guard checks in find_common_ancestor (visited_b.contains(&a) and visited_a.contains(&b) immediately after initial setup). At that point visited_a={a}, visited_b={b}, and a!=b is already guaranteed by the early return.

title: three_way_diff: replace unreachable dead arm
description: Replace (_, None, None) => {} in three_way_diff match with unreachable!() to make the dead arm explicit. The arm can never fire because the early our==thr guard already exits when both are None.

title: check_merge: hoist timeline_is_protected(dest) out of per-path loop
description: timeline_is_protected(dest) returns a loop-invariant value but is called inside the per-ACL loop via path_is_protected call chain. Hoist it before the loop.

title: copy_objects raw-byte note
description: copy_objects in src/repo/mod.rs decodes via get() then re-encodes via put() rather than raw byte copy. Functionally correct today (postcard is deterministic, content-addressed ids stable). Add a comment documenting the determinism assumption. If codec versioning ever changes this approach needs revisiting.

title: ObjectId::from_bytes: rename to clarify it hashes input
description: ObjectId::from_bytes(data: &[u8]) computes a BLAKE3 hash of arbitrary data, but the name implies constructing from the ObjectId's own byte representation (which is ObjectId::new). Consider renaming to hash(), from_data(), or from_content() to avoid confusion. ObjectId::new([u8;32]) is already the right name for the literal-bytes constructor.

title: Integration tests: run T1 scenarios against DiskBackend
description: tests/object_store.rs T1 tests (immutability, dedup, parent preservation, tree round-trip) only use MemoryBackend. The shared helper pattern in the spec should be used so the same scenarios run against DiskBackend too. Low risk since backend is bytes-opaque, but closes the gap.
